title: rumex occidentalis var. tomentellus
intro: Rumex occidentalis var. tomentellus, also known as Western dock, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Western dock include reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of arthritis, as well as supporting the immune system and protecting against oxidative stress. Western dock is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, with some herbalists also using it in salves and creams for topical applications.
